accept.reason: the no-eval lint-ignore comment on line 48 is pre-existing and unchanged; relocating the file under dev/routines/ makes the whole file read as added vs the base, so the check cannot tell the marker is old. Not a new suppression decision. Safe to drop this acceptance once the move has merged.

sharedConstants.what: extractor-agent-done label — the agent's success signal that fires the finalize workflow. The agent ADDS it (agent-prompt-extractor.md), the finalize workflow's trigger guard CONSUMES it (finalize-extractor.yml), and phase2-finalize.sh CLEARS it (and the anomaly hand-offs reference it). Counts include comments.

extractor-agent-ready label — the hand-off trigger for the Claude Code web routine. The prepare workflow's hand-off script ADDS it (handoff-to-agent.sh) and the agent REMOVES it (agent-prompt-extractor.md); the web-routine trigger that consumes it is configured outside this repo. Counts include comments.

extractor-blocked-needs-human label — the human-triage signal added when the automation can't proceed and hands the issue to a person: the agent's bail path (agent-prompt-extractor.md), the finalize anomaly hand-off (phase2-finalize.sh), and the prepare workflow's failure step (auto-implement-extractor.yml) when Phase 1 breaks before the hand-off (e.g. an undownloadable page). Created idempotently before use. Counts include comments.

extractor-request trigger label. Counts include comments AND the substring inside the filename dev/routines/create-extractor/triage-extractor-request.js (the guard counts substrings). Spans: the prepare workflow (header comments, trigger guard, gh issue list filter, the triage-script filename, and the failure step that drops it when handing off to a human), the hand-off script's swap, the popup's request button, and its test.
release zip filename — must match between the build script and the thin workflow stubs that pass it to the Claudinite canon workflows (zip_path in release/daily, zip_name in publish/daily)
extension version — one X.Y.Z token per manifest, kept identical across both files (bumped by the daily-release patcher and the human "bump version" flow); regex so it needs no re-pinning on a bump
